explanation: This command uses diantz.exe to compress a file into a CAB archive stored in an Alternate Data Stream (ADS) of the target file. It aligns with the Sigma rule's detection criteria by including 'diantz.exe', '.cab', and the ADS syntax (':stream.cab').
This command uses diantz.exe with a space between the filename and ADS stream name, which is still valid in Windows. It includes all the required elements for the Sigma rule to detect.
This command uses the equivalent /c flag instead of -c. The rest of the command remains unchanged and meets the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command adds a verbose flag (-v) to the original command. The core behavior remains unchanged, and the Sigma rule will still detect the presence of 'diantz.exe', '.cab', and the ADS syntax.
